# Accessibility reference (WCAG 2.2 AA baseline)

This reference defines baseline accessibility checks required when `pix-styleguides` is applied.

## Scope

Apply these checks to:
- HTML semantics and structure
- text and typography readability
- interactive controls and forms
- keyboard interaction and focus visibility
- motion, contrast, and responsive behavior

## Semantic structure

- Use semantic landmarks (`header`, `main`, `nav`, `aside`, `footer`) where appropriate.
- Keep one `h1` per document and preserve heading hierarchy.
- Prefer semantic containers over generic wrappers.
- Avoid using ARIA roles when native HTML already provides correct semantics.

## Text readability and spacing

- Body text should remain readable at 200% zoom without loss of content or functionality.
- Ensure robust line-height and spacing behavior; avoid fixed-height text containers that clip content.
- Support user style overrides for text spacing (line, paragraph, letter, and word spacing) without overlap/cutoff.
- Avoid long unbroken text blocks with poor line length.

## Color and contrast

- Meet WCAG AA contrast targets:
  - normal text: 4.5:1 minimum
  - large text: 3:1 minimum
  - UI components and graphical objects: 3:1 where applicable
- Do not rely on color alone to communicate state or meaning.
- Ensure focus indicator contrast is visible against adjacent colors.

## Keyboard and focus

- Every interactive element must be reachable and operable via keyboard.
- Preserve logical tab order aligned with visual and semantic order.
- Do not remove focus outlines without equivalent or better replacement.
- Ensure focus visible state is clear and persistent while focused.

## Target size and interaction

- Aim for sufficiently large clickable/touch targets and spacing to reduce accidental activation.
- Avoid dense action clusters with no separation.
- Keep pointer and keyboard interactions consistent for controls.

## Forms and input assistance

- Every input must have an associated label.
- Provide clear required/optional indicators.
- Provide error messages linked to relevant fields.
- Keep instructions visible when errors occur; do not rely only on color.

## Motion and sensory safety

- Respect `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`.
- Avoid auto-playing motion that cannot be paused when it may distract or cause discomfort.
- Avoid flashing patterns that may trigger photosensitive reactions.

## Responsive and reflow checks

- Validate reflow at narrow widths and zoomed views.
- Avoid horizontal scroll for core content unless layout truly requires it.
- Ensure controls remain usable on small screens and touch devices.

## Media and alternatives

- Provide meaningful `alt` text for informative images.
- Use empty `alt` for decorative images.
- Ensure icons with action meaning have accessible names.

## Practical review checklist

1. Structure: semantic landmarks and heading hierarchy are correct.
2. Contrast: text/UI contrast meets AA thresholds.
3. Keyboard: all actions reachable and usable with keyboard only.
4. Focus: visible focus indicator on every interactive control.
5. Text spacing/zoom: content still readable and functional at higher zoom.
6. Targets/forms: labels, errors, and control size are usable.
7. Motion: reduced-motion preferences are respected.

## Reporting format

When auditing, report:
- `issue`: what fails
- `impact`: who is affected and how
- `location`: file and element
- `fix`: concrete compliant change
- `status`: fixed / deferred with rationale

## WCAG 2.2 AA practical thresholds

- Text contrast:
  - normal text: at least 4.5:1
  - large text: at least 3:1
- Non-text contrast for UI controls and icons: at least 3:1 where applicable.
- Reflow and zoom:
  - verify at 200% zoom minimum
  - no critical functionality loss
- Focus and keyboard:
  - all controls keyboard operable
  - visible focus on every interactive control

## Semantic structure examples

Bad:

```html
<div class="header">...</div>
<div class="content">...</div>
<div class="footer">...</div>
```

Preferred:

```html
<header>...</header>
<main>...</main>
<footer>...</footer>
```

## Focus indicator examples

Preferred focus style:

```css
button:focus-visible,
a:focus-visible,
input:focus-visible {
  outline: 0.2rem solid var(--focus-ring-color);
  outline-offset: 0.2rem;
}
```

Avoid:

```css
*:focus {
  outline: none;
}
```

## Form accessibility examples

Preferred:

```html
<label for="email">Email</label>
<input id="email" name="email" type="email" aria-describedby="email-help email-error" />
<p id="email-help">Use your work email.</p>
<p id="email-error" role="alert">Please provide a valid email.</p>
```

## Audit workflow example

1. Run automated check (`axe-core`).
2. Validate keyboard-only flow manually.
3. Validate zoom/reflow manually.
4. Validate color contrast using tooling.
5. Track issue list using the reporting format above.
